name: Panama City — Bella Vista, Panama

visa.notes: Pensionado visa requires $1,000/mo pension income. Extensive retiree discounts.

healthcare.notes: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders; private insurance ~$200-350/mo per person (ASSA / Mapfre / Bupa LatAm / international). Pre-existing exclusions common first 12-24 months. Pensionado 15-25% discount on most medical services. Farmacia Arrocha is the national chain. Excellent specialist access in Panama City: Hospital Punta Pacifica (JCI-accredited, Johns Hopkins-affiliated), Hospital Nacional, Pacifica Salud, Hospital Paitilla, Hospital San Fernando. Anti-VEGF + ophthalmology + cardiology + oncology widely available locally. GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) available via private prescription, ~$200-400/mo.

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: World-class private hospitals including Johns Hopkins-affiliated Punta Pacifica Hospital. Public CSS available.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private facilities; moderate at public

pros: Modern urban amenities and world-class dining
Excellent private healthcare infrastructure
Major international hub with direct flights

cons.text: Hot and humid year-round
Traffic congestion is severe
Higher cost of living than other Panama locations
Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country

sources.title: Numbeo — Crime in Panama City — Bella Vista

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income. Panama City has higher living costs but extensive urban amenities.

name: Coronado, Panama

healthcare.notes: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders; private insurance ~$200-350/mo per person (ASSA / Mapfre / Bupa LatAm / international). Pre-existing exclusions common first 12-24 months. Pensionado 15-25% discount on most medical services. Farmacia Arrocha is the national chain. Centro Médico San Fernando-Coronado satellite + local clinics handle routine care. Panama City hospitals (~50 mi / ~1 hr east via the Pan-American Highway) for advanced specialty care, including JCI-accredited Punta Pacifica. Coronado is the closest beach community to top-tier Panama City medicine.

name: El Valle de Antón, Panama

healthcare.notes: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders; private insurance ~$200-350/mo per person (ASSA / Mapfre / Bupa LatAm / international). El Valle de Antón is a small mountain town with a Centro de Salud (public clinic). Hospital Aquilino Tejeira in Penonomé (~40 min east) handles regional cases; Panama City (~75 mi east) for tertiary care. Anti-VEGF + advanced ophthalmology only in capital.

name: Chitré, Panama

healthcare.notes: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders; private insurance ~$200-350/mo per person (ASSA / Mapfre / Bupa LatAm / international). Pre-existing exclusions common first 12-24 months. Pensionado 15-25% discount on most medical services. Farmacia Arrocha is the national chain. Hospital Cecilio Castillero is the Azuero Peninsula's regional public hospital. Hospital Anita Moreno (private) and Centro Médico Paitilla satellite serve private patients. Tertiary care (cardiology, oncology, complex surgery) typically referred to Panama City (~3 hr northwest).

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

name: David, Panama

healthcare.notes: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders; private insurance ~$200-350/mo per person (ASSA / Mapfre / Bupa LatAm / international). Pre-existing exclusions common first 12-24 months. Pensionado 15-25% discount on most medical services. Farmacia Arrocha is the national chain. Hospital Chiriquí is the regional anchor in David — the largest hospital in Western Panama, with cardiology + general surgery + maternity. Anti-VEGF and routine ophthalmology available locally; complex specialty care typically referred to Panama City (~5-6 hr drive east). David is also Boquete's referral hospital (~30 mi north).

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

name: Bocas del Toro, Panama

healthcare.notes: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders; private insurance ~$200-350/mo per person (ASSA / Mapfre / Bupa LatAm / international). Pre-existing exclusions common first 12-24 months. Pensionado 15-25% discount on most medical services. Farmacia Arrocha is the national chain. Hospital Bocas del Toro on Isla Colón handles primary care and stabilization. Limited specialty services — medevac to David (~6 hr by boat-then-drive via Almirante) or Panama City flight for serious conditions. Anti-VEGF + advanced ophthalmology not available locally; planned trips to Panama City required for chronic eye conditions.

name: Central Valley (San José, Escazú, Santa Ana), Costa Rica

visa.notes: Pensionado: $1,000/mo permanent pension or retirement income (e.g., Social Security), covering a married couple and dependents. Two-year renewable temporary residency; eligible for permanent residency after three years. Renewal requires converting the income through a Costa Rican bank and demonstrating in-country presence (commonly cited as ~4 months/year). Alternative: Rentista visa - $2,500/mo guaranteed income for 2+ years, or a $60,000 deposit in an approved CR bank. CAJA (public health) enrollment is mandatory at ~7-11% of declared income.

healthcare.notes: CCSS (Caja) universal coverage after pensionado/rentista visa, ~7-11% of declared income (~$60-150/mo). Private (INS / international plans) ~$100-250/mo with pre-existing loadings. Anti-VEGF injections at CIMA and Hospital La Católica in San José. GLP-1 ~$300-400/mo private. Pensionado discount 10-25% on most medical services.

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA (public universal healthcare) plus excellent private hospitals (CIMA, Clínica Bíblica). Among best in Latin America.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private; moderate at CAJA public system
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: CAJA covers prescriptions; private insurance also available

pros.text: Best healthcare infrastructure in Central America (CCSS universal coverage; PAHO ranks Costa Rica at or near the top of Latin America in health-system indicators)

sources.title: PAHO — Costa Rica Country Profile (Health Indicators)
Ministerio de Salud Costa Rica — Sistema de salud

pros: Spring-like climate year-round in the valley
Large established expat community in Escazú/Santa Ana

cons.text: Traffic congestion in San José metro area
Higher cost of living than other Costa Rica areas
Air quality issues in urban core
Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: only Costa Rica-sourced income is taxed. Foreign pensions, Social Security, and IRA/investment income are not taxed by Costa Rica. VAT (IVA) is 13%. The mandatory CAJA social-health contribution (~7-11% of declared income) funds public healthcare and is separate from income tax.

name: Lake Chapala / Ajijic, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 in savings. Renewable annually, converts to permanent after 4 years.

healthcare.notes: IMSS public system available to residents (~$500-700/yr fixed, varies by age). Private international plans ~$100-300/mo. Anti-VEGF injections widely available in Mexico City, Guadalajara, Monterrey, and San Miguel; ~$700-1,200/injection at private. GLP-1 via private prescription, ~$200-400/mo. Major retiree expat hubs (San Miguel, Lake Chapala, Mérida) have strong English-speaking specialist networks.

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; excellent private hospitals in Guadalajara (45 min). Local clinics in Ajijic.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at local private clinics; Guadalajara for specialists
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Very affordable; many meds available over the counter

pros: Largest US/Canadian expat community in Mexico
Spring-like climate year-round at lake elevation
Extremely well-established expat infrastructure

cons.text: Can feel like an American bubble
Lake water quality concerns
Limited direct international flights
Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country

sources.title: Numbeo — Crime in Lake Chapala / Ajijic

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents: only Mexican-source income taxed. US pensions and Social Security not taxed by Mexico for temporary residents.

name: Medellín (El Poblado, Laureles), Colombia

visa.notes: Retirement visa requires ~$750/mo pension income (3x minimum wage). Renewable annually. Colombia also offers a digital nomad visa.

healthcare.notes: EPS public system available to residents (Migration Visa M after 5 years for permanent residency). Private international plans ~$80-150/person/mo with pre-existing condition loadings; exclusions common first 6-12 months. Anti-VEGF injections available in Bogotá (Fundación Oftalmológica Nacional), Medellín, Cali. GLP-1 imported, ~$300-450/mo private. Hypertension/diabetes generics widely available at ~10-30% of US prices.

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EPS public system; excellent private hospitals. Medellín is a medical tourism hub with top-rated clinics.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private hospitals; moderate at EPS

Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country

name: Cuenca, Ecuador

visa.notes: Retirement visa requires $1,375/mo income (3x Ecuador minimum wage). Includes discounts on utilities, flights, and entertainment.

healthcare.notes: IESS public system affordable for residents with pensionado/visa, ~$70-90/person/mo. Private international plans ~$80-180/mo. Anti-VEGF injections available in Quito (Hospital Metropolitano), Cuenca, and Guayaquil; ~$1,200-2,000/injection at private clinics. GLP-1 limited availability, ~$300/mo private. Cuenca is a regional medical hub for retirees with strong specialist density.

name: Montevideo, Uruguay

visa.notes: Proof of stable income ~$1,500/mo required. Uruguay offers straightforward residency with path to citizenship.

healthcare.notes: ASSE/FONASA universal coverage for residents (pensioner residency requires legal pension income proof). Private (Hospital Británico, Casa de Galicia, Asociación Española) ~$100-220/mo. Anti-VEGF injections available in Montevideo. GLP-1 partially covered through FONASA for diabetes. Strong public infrastructure; Montevideo is the medical hub.
